notes: Ancient Greek orator known for his polished and fluent extempore speeches, praised by Pliny the Younger.

docs.title: Letter 2003
docs.author: Pliny the Younger
docs.excerpt: … upon Isaeus not only as a wonderfully learned man but as one who possesses a most enviable lot, and you must be made of flint and iron if you do not burn to make his acquaintance. So if there is nothing else to draw you here, if I myself am not a sufficient attraction, do come to hear Isaeus.
